chunks.text: Umesh Vazirani is the Roger A. Strauch Professor of EECS and the co-director of the Berkeley Quantum Computation Center (BQIC). His research interests lie primarily in quantum computing. Education: 1986, Ph.D., Computer Science, UC Berkeley. 1981, B.S., MIT. Research Areas: Theory (THY), Quantum computing, algorithms and complexity theory. Security (SEC). Research Centers: Berkeley Quantum Information and Computation Center (BQIC). Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC).
chunks.question: What are Umesh Vazirani's primary research interests?

chunks.text: Teaching Schedule: CS 170. Efficient Algorithms and Intractable Problems, TuTh 15:30-16:59, Stanley 105. Selected Publications: S. Arora, S. Rao, and U. Vazirani, 'Geometry, flows, and graph-partitioning algorithms,' Communications ACM, vol. 51, no. 10, pp. 96-105, Oct. 2008. L. Orecchia, L. J. Schulman, U. Vazirani, and N. K. Vishnoi, 'On partitioning graphs via single commodity flows,' in Proc. 40th Annual ACM Symp. on Theory of Computing (STOC 2008), New York, NY: The Association for Computing Machinery, Inc., 2008, pp. 461-470.
chunks.question: What course does Umesh Vazirani teach?

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: Foundations of Computer Science (FOCS) Test of Time Award, 2025. ACM SIGECOM Test of Time Award, 2024. Symposium on Theory of Computing (STOC) Test of Time Award, 2023. National Academy of Sciences (NAS) Member, 2018. Delbert Ray Fulkerson Prize, 2012. Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) Fellow, 2005. NSF Presidential Young Investigator (PYI), 1987.
chunks.question: When was Umesh Vazirani elected to the National Academy of Sciences?
